page: Microsoft Azure D2ns_v6 is a network-optimized, general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dnv6 server family. Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture, it features 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with 2 threads per core via Hyper-Threading. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, translating to 4.0 GB per core, and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, but supports Premium Storage for external disk attachments.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance scoring 1665.09 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 26.86 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory subsystem performance is constrained. The stress-ng multi-core metric is poor at 1706.89, and memory bandwidth drops significantly from 134.72 GB/sec for small cached blocks to 17.21 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Passmark CPU and memory scores fall into the weak category, reflecting the limitations of the single physical core configuration.

The primary tradeoff of the D2ns_v6 is its limited parallel processing capacity and low memory bandwidth, which restricts its utility for data-intensive or highly concurrent applications. From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a low-cost entry point for workloads that require dedicated Intel Xeon execution resources without the overhead of high-capacity memory or local storage. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, basic network-optimized routing, single-threaded utility scripts, and small-scale development or testing environments.
description: Microsoft Azure D2ns_v6 is a network-optimized, general-purpose virtual machine within the Dnv6 family. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 2 vCPUs across 1 physical core, 8.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage. Running on Microsoft Hyper-V, the instance delivers average single-core performance, as shown by a stress-ng single-core score of 1665.09 and gzip compression of 26.86 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks are weak, with large-block read bandwidth limited to 17.21 GB/sec. This resource profile makes the instance a cost-effective option for lightweight, single-threaded tasks, basic network routing, and development environments, while being less suitable for memory-intensive or highly parallelized workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2ns_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine featuring a 2-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor, 8.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2ns_v6 is a network-optimized VM with 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, built for light workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, network-optimized virtual machine with dedicated Intel Xeon processing for lightweight single-threaded applications and basic utility tasks.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 2 vCPUs and 1 physical core
8.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Premium Storage capable with no local disk storage bundled
Average single-core performance with weak multi-core scaling
Best suited for lightweight web serving, network routing, and testing
